reviews.review_text:
RDR 2 is a perfect game . 10/10 story , characters , voice acting , visuals , action , sound design , music , songs . You attach to several characters , love them , try to protect them .
I would say Arthur Morgan is one of the best character in the whole gaming as well as pop culture community .
As we proceed in game the stakes gets higher , you start questioning everything and you know where the game is going . Arthur also knows about his fate but then also he goes on . 
This is the story which will stay with me as long as i live .This game makes you feel friendship , betrayal and love . I can never relive this experience again but its ok  .It has given me much i dont want anything else . 
It is one of my favourite games of all time .
I want to write more I want to discuss more but i dont wanna give spoilers . So thats it . Definitely play it once . I guarantee you will not regret . 
Thanks for reading .

Second playthrough
Still can't believe this game even exists
Unrealisticly incredible

In 2019 I played Sekiro: Shadow Die Twice, every RPG I played after felt boring and bland, till I played Red Dead Redemption 2 and met Arthur Morgan. Rockstar has built a stunning world, emotional story, and memorable characters. Towards the end of the game, I was torn between curiosity of how the story unfolds and fear of the journey's end. This is the brilliance of Rockstar's story-telling.

The story revolves around Arthur Morgan, most trusted senior gun of gang leader and outlaw Dutch Van Der Linde set in the wild west in 1899. The game starts by introducing the gang members, epic open world, and setting one of the most intriguing stories in video games in my opinion. Rockstar has created multiple memorable characters with outstanding voice acting and character progression complimented by a captivating world that feels alive and interactive based on your actions through the honor system. Every character feels unique with a lot of personality and Arthur develops distinct relationships with each of them. The campaign is around 50-60 hours long and I still failed to predict what's gunna happen or who am gunna meet, this state of uncertainty and unpredictability results in a much more enjoyable and immersive experience.

Rockstar's attention to detail in RDR2's gameplay took realism to a whole new level and made Arthur Morgan feel like a human, it is noticeable from moving and shooting to your interactions with the world. In addition, the world is full of interesting characters with unique story lines if you want to take a break from the campaign, each of them offers a unique experience and further introduces you to the world's activities. The world is really gorgeous with harmonious music that I often preferred riding instead of fast traveling.

RDR2 is a masterpiece full of charismatic characters, impressive open-world full of life and excitement, engaging gameplay, and slow-burn, emotional, and profoundly tragic story about gang violence in the wild west. This game is unique, and everyone deserves to experience it. Arthur Morgan is the best fictional character in gaming history, in my opinion at least.

By god this is a certified good game. 

RDR2 is a prequel to RDR1, which is a spiritual successor to PS2/XBOX title called Red Dead Revolver. This game sets the stage for the events of RDR1, and deepens the story of it by leagues. You aren't getting the full story of RDR1 without this gem.

We follow Arthur Morgan, down and dirty gunslinger on the run from the law with his mentor's Dutch and Hosea, and their ragtag gang of miscreants, criminals, and ♥♥♥♥♥♥. The year is 1899, and the Wild West is dying. The changing times threaten their way of life, and the "freedom" they so valiantly claim to fight for.

Mechanically, it's similar to other Rockstar games, with a heavy emphasis on realistic physics and weighty actions. The gunplay feels great, punchy and loud just like it should be, but still slow enough to feel like a western shootout, with PLENTY of time inbetween those fights to just enjoy the world itself. It's so artfully crafted that it makes you want to take time out of rootin and shootin to instead brush their horses hair, or enjoy some beans on a nice trail by a fire. Maybe while at camp, you see a herd of Elk, and opt in to do some hunting, scoring an Elk and taking the hide into town to have a new saddle made. 

The game sneakily gets you lost in the open countrysides and winding valleys, constantly distracted by more and more things to do. It's a real treat when you can just *exist* in a game world, and not feel compelled to rush thru or play into a meta. I have so much more to say about the story but fear I can't without spoiling most of it. It's one of the best western stories ever told, and it deserves your time.

This is easily one of the best games I’ve ever played. The graphics still look insane even today, and the world feels more alive than almost any other game. The weather system, water physics, wildlife, and small details are honestly crazy and make everything feel realistic.

The NPCs are amazing too. People react to what you do, remember encounters, and the world never feels empty. Rockstar put so much effort into making the game feel real.

The story is the biggest highlight though. Arthur Morgan is one of the best characters in gaming and the story is emotional, immersive, and unforgettable. Personally I think it’s one of if not the best story ever told in a game.

The only downside is Red Dead Online. Rockstar pretty much abandoned it with very few meaningful updates, which is disappointing because it had so much potential.

Overall, this game is a masterpiece and absolutely worth playing.
Its more than a game, its an experience or journey to the time of outlaws with the best pals you ever need.
Many years of dipping in and out of the game from console to pc, i have finished the story. 10/10 nuff said.

First of all, let me say  this game was one of the best experiences of my life. Pay attention to 'was'. I really can't find any fault with the game itself. But this stupid Rockstar Launcher won't even let me play offline. I honestly don't know what to say anymore. I really hope Rockstar comes to their senses and ditches this ridiculous launcher. This is giving me a real headache🤦

Red Dead Redemption 2 is nothing short of a masterpiece. From the moment you step into the vast, meticulously crafted world of the American frontier, the game pulls you in with its stunning visuals, dynamic weather, and living ecosystem. Every town, forest, and mountain feels alive, and the attention to detail is extraordinary—from the way horses react to terrain to the conversations of NPCs going about their daily lives.

The story is deep, emotional, and unforgettable. Following Arthur Morgan and the Van der Linde gang, you experience loyalty, betrayal, and the struggle to survive in a changing world. The characters are rich and complex, each with their own motivations, and the voice acting and cinematic cutscenes make you truly care about their journey.

Gameplay is equally impressive. The combination of exploration, hunting, combat, and side activities like fishing or poker ensures that no two days in the game feel the same. The honor system adds meaningful choices, shaping how the world reacts to you.

Whether it’s riding across the mountains at sunrise, engaging in a high-stakes shootout, or simply watching the wildlife roam, Red Dead Redemption 2 delivers an immersive experience that is hard to forget. For fans of story-driven open-world games, this is an absolute must-play.

The single player is amazing. Great story, good rhythm, good progression.

The multiplayer? It's a joke. For as big of a company that Rockstar is, it doesn't deserve a single ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ penny from you. They have abandoned the multiplayer for the longest time, the only reason it's not been branded as "the developer stopped updating this game" is because they cycle "triple EXP and dollar events" every few months. It feels like a mobile phone type game, with paywalls everywhere. 15 gold to unlock a role that only really gives you cosmetics, and nothing of note can be earned there. The collector role has been nerfed to the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ ground, so you can't even earn a steady cash income any more. Everything is randomized instead of doing set routes every day to make some money. One of the Arabian horses can only be bought with gold, and half the cosmetics, too. Don't worry, you can do dailies to earn gold - but only a limited amount of dailies. I think you are limited to 9 a day, and you only get a tiny amount of gold. But after a month, you get a multiplier - but if you miss a day? You're back to square one. This game has such extreme potential, but Rockstar has absolutely pulled the rug. This is one of the greatest video game scams of the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ century, I can not recommend this game in the slightest, honestly - not even the singleplayer I can recommend, because they'll take it as a win and earn money. 
Absolute ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ scam.
-5921352368236826/10
A great game with a truly unforgettable story.
Truly the most rootinest-tootinest-shootinest-bootinest-lootinest-grootinest-high falootinest-convolutinest yee-haw game of all time.

Red Dead Redemption 2 could have been one of the greatest online experiences ever made. Instead, in Europe, it has become a circus run by hackers, mod menu clowns, and scammers while Rockstar sits on their hands pretending nothing is wrong.

I cannot recommend this game to anyone in its current state.

The single player is brilliant. The world is beautiful. The atmosphere is incredible. But Red Dead Online? Absolute neglect.

You log in and within five minutes someone is spawning explosions under your horse, teleporting players around the map, stealing legendary pelts, spawning invincible NPCs, crashing sessions, or griefing the entire lobby for laughs.

And reporting them? Completely pointless.

Nothing happens.

The same hackers keep showing up over and over again, ruining lobby after lobby without consequences. Rockstar gives players a report function that feels more like decoration than moderation. It creates the illusion that action is being taken, while these people continue running wild for years.

Meanwhile, on US servers this problem seems far less severe, which makes the European experience feel even more abandoned. It honestly feels like Rockstar simply gave up on moderating this region.

Nothing kills immersion faster than finally tracking a legendary animal for half an hour, only for some pathetic mod menu goblin to vaporize you, kill your horse, and steal the pelt while Rockstar’s anti cheat system takes a nap in the saloon.

Rockstar made billions. BILLIONS. Yet apparently protecting legitimate players from rampant cheating is too much to ask.

Until Rockstar actually starts banning hackers properly, securing lobbies, and protecting normal players from this garbage, I cannot in good conscience recommend Red Dead Online to anyone.

A masterpiece world ruined by complete developer neglect.
playing this game feels like sitting down to watch a 10 season long tv show and then once youve finished it you dont really know what to do with the rest of your life

Having played the first Red Dead Redemption a couple of years ago, I finally got around to experiencing the second - and what an amazing journey it was. The game offers a great storyline, great characters, a great soundtrack and a breathtakingly realistic world that often made me stop and just take in the scenery. There is an endless list of things to do that will easily consume hundreds of hours.

The middle part of the game felt a bit repetitive in my opinion, partly because riding your horse takes up a significant portion of your playtime. Strangely enough, the epilogue ended up being my personal highlight. That’s probably due to how invested I am in John Marston's story from the first game. However, this takes absolutely nothing away from Arthur Morgan, who is without a doubt one of Rockstar's most complex and interesting protagonists. I especially loved how the choices you make through the game's "honor system" directly shape his storyline.

Honestly, this is a masterpiece that every gamer needs to play at least once in their life.

This game would be the easiest 10/10 of all time if it werent for Rockstar's horrendous launcher. I am unable to register an account to play the Singplayer.... After numerous attempts I had to use mobile data on my phone to register an account > SAID ACCOUNT DOESNT WORK WHEN I TRY TO LOG IN SAYING THIS ACCOUNT DOESNT EXIST WHEN I AM CLEARLY LOGGED INTO IT ON MY PHONE. 

AVOID BUYING THIS LIKE THE PLAGUE IT IS AN UTTER MESS OF LAUNCHER RIDDLED WITH BUGS....

Shame such a great game is left to die.
Best game I’ve ever played. Please play it if you haven’t already. The story is good, the graphics look amazing, and the gameplay is great.Open world is big, and there's a lot to discover.

The Social Club launcher requirement is horrendous.
I have a 15-16 y/o social club account from back when I played on XB360, with the same username I have now.
So I obviously want to still use that account. I reset the password, which worked flawlessly, but then the system also wanted me to verify (why... it's the same email..) my account again via an email code. Except that damned email either didn't show up at all, OR it showed up way too late, and it arrived after the 15min expiration timer was already up.

I didn't even want to play right away. It was two hours ago when I thought "hm, yeah, lemme get my account details ready, reset PW, and make sure everything is ready for later/tomorrow". I reset my PW, which that email arrived instantly, changed PW and then tried to log in. Waited for the code.. and waited.. and waited.. resent the code.. waited.. waited.. waited...

By the time the first code showed up I had already requested another code to be sent again, but it would've already been outside the 15min window anyway. And then the same story with the second code. Wouldn't show up for ages. I resent a few times after that and none of those ever showed up.

And now I am simply locked out. For how long? Who knows.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Got in the morning after. Adjusted settings, ran benchmark. Crashed (froze the PC) twice.
Odd bugs like needing to keep triple buffer vsync enabled (while vsync is off) to not have a 60fps lock. Other odd things like unresponsive menus and a bug I've already forgotten by now.
Game keeps randomly crashing and alt-tabbing is a no-no, since there's an about 1 in 5 chance for it to just take the entire ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ system down and requiring me to press the power button on my case to restart the PC.
The game's key config menu is a mess and clunky. The game's overall controls are clunky and messy.
The graphics, the one thing that always gets praise, are underwhelming. The game's barely looking better than an early 8th gen open world game.
The graphics are very blurry with TAA, even at 1.5-2x render scale. There's also noticeable ghosting around your character when you turn the camera. This happens regardless of if TAA is enabled or not. With TAA off, even with MSAA and FXAA and/or render scale above 1x, the game remains ugly. And with TAA off there's seemingly more pop-in happening.

Oh and yeah, the bug I forgot was about the keyboard. If you have RDR2 open and alt-tab, to idk.. type sth on Discord, the typing is super laggy. I googled it and there's an odd fix where two settings need to be enabled in the launcher.


I loved RDR"1", I got it on release. Great game, basically just GTA4 but Wild West. And had I played this game when I was a kid I am sure I would've loved it too, but my view on quality has changed and playing this game on PC is just a headache I don't really want to deal with.
The constant freezing up my PC while trying to alt-tab, the random out of GPU memory errors, when I have 10GB and the game's using like 4000MB, with 4500MB allocated or sth.

Could this be driver-related? I am on 560.70. Sure, could be. But I don't really care. Every other game works, old or new. Could it be the undervolting curve? Maybe. Again, every other game works fine, this undervolt is stable with anything else I play. Could it be the CPU OC? Maybe. The fact I am mixing and matching two Corsair RAM kits? Maybe.

Maybe there's something the game simply dislikes. In the end, I don't care.

Feel free to comment "user error" and then go look up all of these issues on the forums. There's plenty of people complaining about this and that and God knows what kinds of issues. People getting DCed from their online session, crashes, etc.
It's clear that the game's been abandoned by Rockstar and that they don't care. They couldn't even be assed to update DLSS. If you don't want to use DLSS 2 you'll need to either override in the nvidia app or manually swap out dll files.
Not that DLSS or DLAA could save this game, I think. But I sure as ♥♥♥♥ would love to have DLAA in this game, since everything else looks bad. I really cannot understate how disappointed I am in the graphics. If I step scoot my chair back and look at the game, yeah, it's "fine", but sitting at the desk the game's just blurry. And without TAA, again, what I wrote.


Meh. Disappointed.


Second edit: Googled the out of memory crash. Some people say overlays in general can cause it, others say even just having Afterburner open can cause it.
Either way, a broken game. I use Afterburner for fan control since I got my 1060 in 2017. Never had issues this bad. BF3 will act up if, I recall, RT is open? But only at launch. Either AB or RT. But that game lets you launch AB after the fact and it'll work. Here I've seen posts of people still crashing regardless.
Also weird considering people benchmark the game on YT with the RT overlay and there's no issues for them.

Again, anyone doubting me just google my issues listed, they're not exclusive to my system, there's plenty of posts complaining.
Terrible PC port. If you have to fight with a game to do something as simple as just getting it to play in fullscreen, from a dev as big as Rockstar, after how many years? Yeah, no...

I am not the IT guy fixing their system and shame on Steam not having the guts to tell them to fix their server problems or else

I would say Arthur Morgan is one of the best character in the whole gaming as well as pop culture community .
As we proceed in game the stakes gets higher , you start questioning everything and you know where the game is going . Arthur also knows about his fate but then also he goes on .

To say that this is one of the best games is to say nothing. The scale of the game will amaze any gamer. The plot is more than exciting, the open world provides plenty of opportunities for entertainment, the characters are well written and the developers are not afraid to raise many very controversial topics in the game. The graphics are incredibly beautiful and detailed. If the game really draws you in, then it’s better not to look at the counter of hours played, so as not to think later about how much you could have done in life during this time.
Might be the best video game ever created since Kingdom Hearts 2 and Super Mario Bros. 2

Red Dead Redemption 2 — одна из тех игр, из которых не хочется выходить уже через час. Обычно в некоторых играх через час уже тянет зайти в помойки по типу CS 2 или Dota 2, чтобы спокойно подеградировать. А тут наоборот — хочется выполнять даже побочные квесты, потому что они проработаны почти на уровне основной сюжетной ветки.

Сюжет не кажется скучным или слишком прямым: в игре много интересных персонажей, и у каждого есть своя история, а не просто болванчик по типу «мужик, который стоит у костра и курит». Радует и графика, и живая мимика героев, и то, насколько много всего можно делать в открытом мире. Мир ощущается не как пустая карта с вопросиками, а как реально живое место.

В отличие от многих других AAA-проектов, RDR 2 чувствуется цельной и продуманной. Тут нет ощущения, что половина бюджета ушла на трейлер, вторая — на маркетинг, а разработчикам оставили сухарики, колу и дедлайн на вчера. Видно, что деньги и время пошли именно на проработку мира, персонажей и мелких деталей.

Отдельно хочу выделить мимику, с которой даже в более современных играх часто бывают проблемы. На лица персонажей приятно смотреть, эмоции хорошо считываются, и даже вне кат-сцен герои не стоят деревянными манекенами, а занимаются своими делами и реагируют на происходящее.

Из игр Rockstar я раньше проходил только GTA: Vice City и San Andreas, но даже по ним видно, как студия росла. Улучшалась не только графика, но и постановка сцен, кат-сцены, спецэффекты и общий уровень кинематографичности. В RDR 2 это уже ощущается не просто как игра, а как огромный фильм, в котором тебе ещё и дали управление.

Пока это лучшая игра, в которую я играл. По насыщенности мира, кат-сценам, графике, музыке, проработке персонажей, деталям и сюжету RDR 2 для меня пока занимает первое место.

10 проебанных шляп во время перестрелок из 10.
Fantastic game. Absolutely love it, but the online bugs are quite annoying. Super frequent crashes. You can really feel the neglect from Rock Star :(

Red Dead Redemption 2 is not just a game, it’s an experience. Arthur Morgan is easily one of the best protagonists I’ve ever seen in gaming, and the story is emotional, immersive, and unforgettable from start to finish.

The world feels alive in a way that almost no other game does. You can spend hours just riding around, exploring towns, hunting, talking to NPCs, or admiring the scenery. The amount of detail is honestly insane.

The gameplay is slower and more realistic than most modern games, which might not be for everyone, but that’s exactly what makes it so immersive. The soundtrack, graphics, and attention to detail are still incredible even years later.

If you enjoy story-driven games with amazing characters and a massive open world, this is a must-play.

Masterpiece by Rockstar Games

buy it, on sale or not, it is amazing.

What a stunning game I just loved how it looks very good and it as allot of small details that make it even better.
Story wise the game is also good but I wouldnt say perfect the beginning is prett boring but afterwards it just gets so much better.
The gameplay inst the best but the fact that the game has such a goated story and graphics that it balances it out.
1 issues that I found annoying is that while riding horses when you tap a to go left you just fly to the left wich is kinda annoying I dont know if its meant to be like this bc you are riding a horse wich can be unpredictable.
Overall solid game get ot on sile for like 8 bucks long story like 40 hours

[h3] Good game but the many bugs ruin it! [/h3]

First of all I want to say that this game has an amazing story, very satisfying gameplay and graphics which can hold up even to today's standards. By all accounts it is an amazing game and I really like it.

[h3] HOWEVER [/h3]

The many and sometimes severe bugs pretty much ruin the game and Rockstar has decided to not patch  [b] ANY   [/b] of them! Many of the bugs [b] have existed since LAUNCH in 2016!!! [/b]
For example there is a game breaking bug in the mission where you confront Emmet Granger, a former gunslinger, and as soon as you blow up the pile of pig manure the game crashes. You have to switch the render pipeline from Vulkan to DX12 to watch the cutscene BUT this causes its own issue where the game randomly throws you a "out of VRAM" error - which makes no sense because the game was loading up and didn't fill 1GB on my RX 6800 16GB...

The next game breaking bug is a ranomly occuring "Unknown Error FFFF" which crashes the game instantly.

Among the less severe bugs, which don't instacrash your game, are NPCs and random encounters, which just break and prevent regular progress. The escaped prisoner for example tells us the location of home invasion oppurtunities however the second encounter was bugged for me where he thanked me after I shot his shackles but then he just followed me around instead of telling me what's up.

Another annoying bug is that the animation can freeze for minutes when setting up camp preventing you from cooking up supplies or moving around. You literally gotta sit there and wait for [b] MINUTES [/b] until the game reacts when this happens.

These issues pretty much ruin the enjoyment and fun the game otherwise brings. And no, it is not my PC because I'm getting north of 110 FPS up to 200 FPS depending on the location and what's going on.
The game is also installed on a fast Samsung SSD and I have already tried to verify game files.

The bugs have existed for nearly a decade because I looked them up and found 10 year old threads about them on reddit.
This game is a masterpiece. If GTAV and RDR are games, this is a work of art. Only bad thing is riding horses, otherwise, incredible.

Runs even on a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ surprisingly enough
good game, worth getting

!!Contains spoilers!!
I don’t think I've ever played such a beautiful game before. 
I started out with a very neutral opinion, with kinda low expectations. But my mind was blown(Sean) 
The story gets you hooked so fast, I was immediately interested to get further into the game. You can clearly see the time and effort put into the game. The main story is so beautiful, but also so gut wrenching. With all the happy moments and laughs, there are heart crushing moments too. But it really makes the main story so amazing. 
Other than the main story, there are also so many small stories. You easily spend hundreds of hours and still find something new. I love to just go in and ride around too, everything is so ethereal, the graphics are a real work of art. Dont even get my started on all the characters, there is such a depth to them all. You need to look at all the small details to really understand everyone and why their actions are like they are. But they are all so beautifully written. 
I haven't quite gotten around to my 2nd play through, since I'm lowkey still mourning my first one, but it's definitely a game I could replay again and again. I will always recommend this game, it's really a masterpiece.

Wow... I'm speechless, this is one of the best video games I've ever played. Masterpiece of both storytelling, Environment, characters and everything else that this game has. This game also made me cry so hard too, more than LADG:TMWEHN and it was worth it.

I love Red Dead Redemption 2!

good game. I liked it very much. The shooting sections in missions were fun (only thanks to dead eye ability), and the open world was good as well. Story was great as well, like everyone says, including me. The only minun is that it's slowburn early in the game, and the movement and controls Felt kind of clunky at times.
However, honestly...i still prefer Witcher 3.
9/10 i think..

i just cant fathom how good this game is the best storytelling game i played so far john was my fav character i thought nothing can topple him from that spot but my god arthur morgan was just damn too good basically im a simp for this game gonna do my 4th playthrough in upcoming times contender for best game of all time

Hands down the best story game ive played. The story is unbelievable. It really gets a hold and doesnt let go. It takes you for a ride through the wild west and makes you feel everything from absolutely free and peaceful, to angry and sad. The whole world is detailed to the point it feels alive. Not much I can say that wasn't already said for years. This game either clicks with you or not. If it does, like it did me, 8 years late to the party, it very clearly worms it's way into a Top5 games of all time list. If you don't like the slower pace compared to other action games, it may just be frustrating and you'll wonder why the hype is. For me, since I finally got a computer from this decade and could actually play it, breathe it in, take it slow, inhabit the world, *be* Arthur Morgan for a few dozen (at least) hours? Yes. This game very much lives up to the hype. But if you don't have the time or willingness to take it slow and just... exist in the world? Yeah, I can tell why a lot of people bounced off of it. That being said, it was easily my game of the decade, even if I played it in the wrong decade.

DONT BUY RDR2 ONLINE. 
There are few games I have loved and still love as the whole RDR2. Unfortunately the online game is unplayable. Rock Star are doing something with their servers. So you are constantly being thrown out of the game and can’t reconnect. First I thought it was because they didn’t maintenance the servers, but according to PVP Cat, they are running some tests. Still, it’s a mystery that rock star is still selling the online game. I recommend the RDR2 as the game. Not the online version.

I honestly regret not discovering Red Dead Redemption 2 earlier. I’ve been playing for about a month now, and it’s the first game in a long time that completely pulled me in. I haven’t played hundreds of games, so I can’t compare it to everything out there, but for me personally, this is the game that pulled me in the most so far.
The attention to detail is unbelievable. Every place feels alive, and the landscapes are beautiful.
There are hackers sometimes in the online mode, and some can be annoying, but surprisingly some are actually chill and helpful too. Either way, it never ruined the experience for me because the game itself is just that enjoyable.

Red Dead Redemption 2 is one of the greatest video games ever made because it combines emotional storytelling, incredible realism, and one of the most immersive open worlds in gaming history. Developed by Rockstar Games
, the game follows Arthur Morgan and the Van der Linde gang during the final days of the Wild West, and what starts as an outlaw adventure slowly becomes a heartbreaking story about loyalty, freedom, change, and redemption. Arthur Morgan stands out as one of gaming’s best protagonists thanks to the phenomenal performance by Roger Clark, who gives the character emotional depth and realism that make him feel human rather than fictional. The world itself is astonishingly detailed, with living towns, dynamic wildlife, changing weather, realistic horse mechanics, and random encounters that make every journey feel unique. Simple activities like riding through forests at sunset, fishing near a river, or listening to gang members talk around a campfire create a level of atmosphere that few games can match. Although the gameplay is intentionally slow and realistic, with lengthy animations and heavy movement, that design choice is exactly what makes the experience feel authentic and immersive for many players. The graphics remain stunning even years after release, while the soundtrack and sound design perfectly capture the loneliness and beauty of the dying frontier. Despite some flaws like restrictive mission design and the underdeveloped Red Dead Online mode, the game succeeds because it creates a genuine emotional connection between the player, the world, and its characters. By the end, the gang feels like family, the camp feels like home, and Arthur’s journey becomes unforgettable. More than just a western action game, RDR2 is an emotional masterpiece that proves video games can deliver storytelling and atmosphere on the same level as the best films and novels.

This is one of those games that will turn you into a different person by the end of it.

It's too bad Rockstar is filled with fools and the game randomly decides my "social club account is not entitled" to play the game I payed money for and have over 200 hours in. Red Dead Redemption 2 (RDR2) is widely considered a masterpiece of the open-world genre, though it is famously "slow-burn." It’s less of a traditional action game and more of a high-fidelity outlaw simulator.

I seriously find the online activation requirement annoying. Once in a while makes sense. But every single time I want to play it needs to go online? 😳 The beginning of the game is quite slow, so much dialogue you don't yet care about, and an hour in I still hadn't found anything particularly interesting, it feels like I'm just doing stuff. Perhaps a "modern games problem", but it feels less like a series of challenges & more a series of chores. Like the most mundane things, picking up things, opening cupboards or hunting, every step is so painfully slow & tedious I end up just falling asleep. Beautiful visuals, and it is probably a great game overall, but so far I'm put off by the online roadblocks & the slow pace.

The game is good but if you ever lose access to the Rockstar Account that you are made to create in order to play the game it's over, they are simply no help at all.  I provided my steam ID, transaction date and ID, and other info asked for and they simply said we can not help you.

It is just the best game ever made. Don't ask me why but I have around 300 hours in one safe game and I'm still not finished because this game is so immersive, the story is so great, the graphics are still better than most modern games and it's great on Steam Deck.

Beautiful game, terrific graphics, heavy storyline, easy to navigate gameplay. The only complication I would consider is the long horse riding. Sometimes you need to ride through the whole map, it's boring & time-consuming, despite the beautiful nature around.

Not much to say that hasn't already been said. I bounced off this game twice before finally committing to it and I'm glad I did. The story is great, as is the world building, freedom, visuals, and overall feel of being a cowboy in a gang during this time period. It takes a while to get used to controlling Arthur, but once you do it becomes second nature. I can see why some may be turned off by the slow pace and there isn't really a way around that, but if you can hang up that hat then it's worth it to enter this world. Performance on my computer (upper mid-range aging CPU / high-end GPU in 2026) is fantastic and I only played with a wireless Xbox One controller.

*Edit - I forgot to mention that the amount of time that you spend with your horse was a surprising welcome to me. I knew that horses were the means of transportation during that time but I did not expect to spend so much time taking care of them, making sure they were clean, fed, happy, etc. It was not a burden at all, the horse animations and sounds are great; I treated them better than Arthur to be honest!

Honestly, the game itself is amazing in my opinion. The problem is Rockstar's pathetic support department.  I bought the game, linked my steam account to my rockstar account, downloaded their launcher and a couple months later several of my accounts were compromised. After getting my emails and passwords changed, when i went to log in and play, the hacker had turned on 2 factor authentication. all attempts to login send a code the their email. Several attempts at getting help with this from Rockstar have resulted in nothing but frustration. So far, as of now, i have a game i paid for but can not play.
The save system is totally moronic. Nuked the game from the account.

I should have done more research before purchasing this game. I didn't realize the online part of the game was open world pvp (full of bullies/cheaters/etc and the game creator doesn't care)! Thankfully I only spent $20. I'll never purchase/play another product created by Rockstar!

People that say this game plays like a movie are entirely right. This game is a cinematic masterpiece in the best way possible. If you're willing to take your time with a game's narrative, you will love this. Not to mention, there still is a very high amount of action in this game, just isn't happening 24/7. Top complaint though, ♥♥♥♥ Saint Denis. I don't entirely hate the place, but I tried hard to not hold a bounty during the game and they made it incredibly difficult there.
For as good as the game is, I'd like the main story missions to stop being so suffocatingly linear.

Keep in mind before reading that the review doesn't concern the  game. It is on of the best games i've ever played and i'm truly saddened to write this review. Unfortunately, my old Rockstar Account was created a long time ago with an email address that has since been deleted, making it impossible for me to access the client and thus playing the game. I’ve delayed writing this review for a long time because it’s hard to put into words what Rockstar actually achieved here.
i have dedicate over 1k hours on my xbox.
Most games are built to be fun distractions. Red Dead Redemption 2 feels like a living, breathing ecosystem that you just happen to be allowed to exist in.

The World: It ruins other open-world games for you. If you shoot a bird, a coyote will come eat the carcass later. If you don't wash your clothes, townspeople will treat you like a pariah. You can spend three real-world hours just fishing, hunting, and watching the weather change across the valleys, and it never feels like "grinding."

Arthur Morgan: Easily one of the greatest protagonists ever written in fiction, period. His growth, his internal conflicts, and his loyalty are so masterfully handled that by the end of the journey, you don't feel like you played a character—you feel like you lost a friend.

The Pace: It's slow. Deliberately slow. It forces you to slow down, match its rhythm, and actually live in the late 1890s.

It’s been months since the credits rolled, and I still catch myself listening to the soundtrack on my commute. It’s not just a game; it's a monumental achievement in digital storytelling.

Buy it. Play it. Don't rush it. And keep some tissues nearby for Chapter 6.

10/10. Arthur, you're a good man.

Between the hidden easter eggs, emergent challenges, and dynamic missions, any player could easily lose 2,000 hours to this world without ever feeling the hours pass. And even if you spent every one of those hours simply standing still - just soaking and taking in the scenery - Red Dead Redemption 2 would still leave you hungry for more.

Look for the original PS4 version of this game if you want to fully own this game.

This version has the annoying Rockstar Games Launcher attached to it, making it extremely difficult to play as it has to do multiple online checks to verify you own the game despite me paying for this and having it present in my library. I bought this game to own it on Steam, not on the Rockstar games launcher. Why is this game making me install another launcher on top of Steam? This makes the game sluggish for no good reason whatsoever. You have my money, what more do you want? 

Last thing, but this game's missions are extremely scripted and does not let you complete missions the way you want to do it. As great as the story is, it becomes very frustrating having to battle with the game's logic/scripting when you decide that you can complete the mission objective more efficiently but the game decides, "No, you failed because you abandoned (insert character or objective here)".

I'm probably one of the few people that doesn't like RDR. I'm sure the game is fun for most folks, but I'm not a fan of westerns. Games like GTA, The Witcher, and Cyberpunk are all high enjoyable for me, but I've never really gotten into Old West themes. This is very much a western, so if you are neutral or like those settings, this game is excellent. If you don't however, it is kind of hard to get past the stereotypical settings, character archetypes, and gameplay. I'm sure the story is fascinating, it's just not for me.

Rating: 10/10

After spending over 320 and 34 in this account hours in the world of Red Dead Redemption 2, I can confidently say that this is not just a game; it is a profound artistic achievement. Rockstar Games didn't just build a map; they crafted a living, breathing world that feels more real than any other virtual experience I have ever encountered.

Why RDR2 stands unmatched:

Unparalleled Storytelling: The journey of Arthur Morgan is a Shakespearean tragedy that sticks with you long after the credits roll. It is deeply emotional, mature, and perfectly paced.

Immersive Detail: The level of detail is staggering—from the way the environment reacts to your presence, to the realistic wildlife behavior and the incredibly complex character interactions. Everything serves a purpose.

Technical Perfection: Even years after its release, the graphics, lighting, and sound design remain the industry benchmark. The world is breathtaking, whether you are traversing snowy peaks or dusty plains.

The "Living" World: Unlike many open-world games that feel empty, RDR2 feels alive. You can lose yourself for hours just by interacting with the world, hunting, or simply watching the sunset from your horse.

Final Verdict:
Many games are fun, and some are great, but Red Dead Redemption 2 is in a league of its own. It is the pinnacle of interactive storytelling and world-building. It is, without a doubt, the most complete and perfect game in the history of the medium.

If you haven't played it yet, you aren't just playing a game; you are missing out on one of the greatest cultural experiences of our time.
